meta.description: A meditation app connecting users with live guided sessions — just started

project.name: Mindful Match
project.description: A meditation app that uses AI to match users with live guided sessions matching their mood and goals.
project.primaryGoal: Validate that busy professionals will pay for live meditation sessions

mvvStatement.mission: Make personalized meditation accessible to everyone, anywhere
mvvStatement.vision: A world where mental wellness is as routine as physical fitness
founderAssumptions: Users find existing meditation apps too generic

project.conceptIntake: I want to build a meditation app that uses AI to match users with live guided sessions based on their current mood, stress level, and goals. Unlike Calm and Headspace which offer pre-recorded content, Mindful Match connects users with live instructors in real-time, creating accountability and personalization. Target users are busy professionals aged 25-45 who feel generic meditation apps don't stick. Revenue model: $14.99/month subscription.

personas.name: Busy Professional (30-45)
personas.primaryPain: Generic meditation apps feel impersonal and lack accountability — no one notices when you skip a session
personas.context: Works 50+ hr/week, high stress, tried Headspace/Calm but didn't maintain habit

personas.name: Wellness Seeker (25-35)
personas.primaryPain: Wants meditation to address specific needs (anxiety, focus, sleep) but doesn't know which format works best
personas.context: Active on wellness social media, willing to spend on premium experiences

offers.name: Mindful Match Premium
offers.valueProposition: Live, AI-matched meditation sessions with real instructors tailored to your current state — never a generic session again

offers.name: Corporate Wellness Program
offers.valueProposition: Employee meditation benefits with live group sessions, progress tracking, and stress analytics for HR teams

hypotheses.title: Users feel current meditation apps are too generic
hypotheses.statement: Busy professionals who have tried Calm or Headspace report that pre-recorded content lacks the personalization needed to maintain a daily habit beyond 2 weeks.

riskRank.rationale: Core value proposition — if users are satisfied with existing apps, there is no reason to switch

hypotheses.title: Live instructors improve retention vs recorded
hypotheses.statement: Users who meditate with a live instructor at least twice per week maintain the habit 3x longer than users who use only pre-recorded content.

riskRank.rationale: The entire business model depends on live instruction being measurably better

hypotheses.title: Professionals will pay $14.99/month
hypotheses.statement: Target users currently spend at least $10/month on wellness apps and will pay $14.99 for live-session personalization.

riskRank.rationale: Revenue depends entirely on this price point being acceptable

hypotheses.title: AI mood matching improves session outcomes
hypotheses.statement: Users who are matched to sessions via AI mood assessment report 40% higher satisfaction than random assignment.

riskRank.rationale: Technical moat depends on this being true

hypotheses.title: Companies will buy corporate wellness licenses
hypotheses.statement: HR departments at mid-size tech companies (200-1000 employees) have budget for wellness benefits and will allocate $500+/month for employee meditation access.

riskRank.rationale: B2B revenue could significantly improve unit economics

pmfAssessment.explanation: No evidence collected yet. Start with a concept intake to generate assumptions, then run interviews to validate the core desirability hypothesis.
